Subagent Contract Testing

When designing skills that dispatch fresh-context subagents, define the return contract as a strict JSON object emitted on stdout. Stub the subagent at its boundary by reading a fixture file from an environment variable in tests. This lets the deterministic shell envelope exercise every verdict path (novel, conflict, match, reject, fetch_failed, malformed) in CI without depending on the network or the live subagent's reasoning quality.

Concretely: route a CJ_IMPROVE_QUEUE_VERDICT_FILE env var through the shell envelope. When set, skip the WebFetch + Agent dispatch and feed the stub directly to the apply step. Preflight gates still fire so the Darwin check and dirty-TODOS.md check stay covered.

Trim source quotes to under 200 bytes and wrap them in HTML comments so they cannot be misread as renderable markdown. Layer this defense with a default-on source-domain allowlist to neutralize regex-injection paths.
